Document:
# Capital Crimes These crimes are of extreme severity. All of these crimes will be punished by permanent confinement and demotion.
## Jailbreak/Escape To escape, assist in an escape, attempt escape, or be willfully and knowingly broken out of a cell.
## Attempted Murder Attempting to murder a person but failing to do so. If the evidence shows that the arrested person was clearly trying to kill someone with ill intent but failed in the action itself.
## Murder Killing someone with malicious intent. This includes Synthetic units. The charge applies even if the victim is later revived.
## Terrorism The use or threat of violence against non-combatants or critical infrastructure with the intent to intimidate, coerce, or achieve political, ideological, or religious objectives.
## Sedition To engage in actions or refuse to follow orders to overthrow or usurp the legitimate command structure. Creating a massive threat to the ship without the knowledge and approval of the command falls under sedition.
## Espionage The covert collection of sensitive or classified information with the intent to distribute that information to an enemy force willingly.
## Desertion Refusing to carry out the duties essential to one’s post or abandoning one's post without authorisation, without intent to return. (Retreating from the planet when the FOB is breached is not Desertion; refusing to return when ordered is).
## Crimes against humanity To engage in actions that violate human rights or are otherwise heinous acts against human life. Examples are torture, cannibalism and forced infection of any kind, which may not be stacked on top of a Geneva Violation.
## Violation of the Geneva Conventions Engaging in actions that violate the [textlink="Geneva Conventions of 12th August 2106" link="AU14UCMJGENCON"], may not be stacked on top of Crimes against humanity.
